title: Loops
beginnerExplanation: Loops allow you to execute a block of code multiple times automatically, avoiding repetitive manual coding.
intermediateExplanation: If you want to spawn 100 enemies, writing "spawn_enemy()" 100 times is inefficient. Loops automate repetitive tasks.

realWorldAnalogy.title: A Laps Counter
realWorldAnalogy.description: Running around a track: "For every lap from 1 to 5, drink water." You don't need a separate instruction for each lap, just one instruction that repeats.

mentalModel: Running around a track: "For every lap from 1 to 5, drink water." You don't need a separate instruction for each lap, just one instruction that repeats.
visualDiagram:
graph TD
  Start --> Process[Loops]
  Process --> End
executionWalkthrough: 2. Scopes and local namespaces are verified.
3. Target variables are allocated or updated.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that range(3) yields 0, 1, 2 (it stops BEFORE the given number).
Modifying a list while looping over it.

debuggingMission.scenario: An adventurer is trying to use Loops but hit a common pitfall: Forgetting that range(3) yields 0, 1, 2 (it stops BEFORE the given number).
debuggingMission.explanation: Make sure to follow the best practices: Use "for" loops when you know how many times to repeat.

practiceQuestions.question: What numbers will range(2) generate?
practiceQuestions.explanation: range(n) generates numbers starting from 0 up to n-1.

challengeQuestions.question: What is the correct keyword or operator most commonly associated with Loops?
hints: Look at the syntax summary of the topic.
Check standard keyword usage in Python.

projectExercise.title: Mastering Loops
projectExercise.description: Implement a clean snippet demonstrating the use of Loops.
requirements:
Write a snippet utilizing the syntax: for item in collection:
    # code

I can avoid Forgetting that range(3) yields 0, 1, 2 (it stops BEFORE the given number)..

simplifiedExplanation: Loops allow you to execute a block of code multiple times automatically, avoiding repetitive manual coding.

retentionSystem.revision1: Define the core syntax of Loops.
retentionSystem.revision2: Name one common mistake when using Loops.

developerMode.industryUsage: Loops is widely used in standard library scripts and enterprise Python programs.
developerMode.performanceConsiderations: Optimized by standard compiler rules in modern Python environments.
bestPractices: Use "for" loops when you know how many times to repeat.
Use "while" loops when repeating until a condition changes.
Avoid infinite loops in while statements.
antiPatterns: Forgetting that range(3) yields 0, 1, 2 (it stops BEFORE the given number).
Modifying a list while looping over it.
developerMode.architectureNotes: Interpreted and executed in the primary application call loop.
